items.title: Andy Burnham takes the reins as U.K.'s 7th prime minister in a decade
items.standfirst: Having been asked by King Charles III to form a new government, former Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham has become Britain's 7th prime minister in a decade.
items.content: Having been asked by King Charles III to form a new government, former Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham has become Britain's 7th prime minister in a decade.

items.title: Andy Burnham speaks for first time as Britain's new prime minister
items.standfirst: Newly minted British Prime Minister Andy Burnham addressed crowds at 10 Downing Street after a meeting with King Charles III. CBS News' Imtiaz Tyab joins to discuss Burnham's speech.
items.content: Newly minted British Prime Minister Andy Burnham addressed crowds at 10 Downing Street after a meeting with King Charles III. CBS News' Imtiaz Tyab joins to discuss Burnham's speech.

items.title: 'A circuit-breaker moment': Watch Andy Burnham's first speech as PM in full
items.standfirst: Andy Burnham spoke outside Downing Street after he replaced Keir Starmer as prime minister and leader of the Labour party.
items.content: Andy Burnham spoke outside Downing Street after he replaced Keir Starmer as prime minister and leader of the Labour party.

items.title: Andy Burnham becomes UK prime minister after meeting King Charles
items.standfirst: New Labour leader takes over from Keir Starmer and will head to Downing Street to appoint his cabinetUK politics live – latest updatesAndy Burnham has formally become the UK prime minister after meeting King Charles at Buckingham Palace, the fourth person to hold the role in less than four years and the sixth within a decade.Burnham, who took over from Keir Starmer as leader of the Labour party on Friday, accepted the appointment as prime minister shortly after Starmer had gone to the palace to resign to the monarch. Continue reading...
items.content: New Labour leader takes over from Keir Starmer and will head to Downing Street to appoint his cabinetUK politics live – latest updatesAndy Burnham has formally become the UK prime minister after meeting King Charles at Buckingham Palace, the fourth person to hold the role in less than four years and the sixth within a decade.Burnham, who took over from Keir Starmer as leader of the Labour party on Friday, accepted the appointment as prime minister shortly after Starmer had gone to the palace to resign to the monarch. Continue reading...

items.title: 'I go with a smile': Watch Keir Starmer's last speech as prime minister in full
items.standfirst: Starmer spoke in front of an applauding crowd on Downing Street.
items.content: Starmer spoke in front of an applauding crowd on Downing Street.

items.title: Andy Burnham becomes seventh British prime minister in a decade
items.standfirst: The new Labour Party leader Andy Burnham is set to replace Keir Starmer as British prime minister, inheriting a country beset by low wages and internal strife.
items.content: The new Labour Party leader Andy Burnham is set to replace Keir Starmer as British prime minister, inheriting a country beset by low wages and internal strife.

items.title: LIVE: Andy Burnham appointed UK PM, delivers first speech at 10 Downing St
items.standfirst: The new leader takes over from Keir Starmer just two years after outgoing PM led Labour to a landslide election victory.
items.content: The new leader takes over from Keir Starmer just two years after outgoing PM led Labour to a landslide election victory.

items.title: Andy Burnham becomes U.K. prime minister
items.standfirst: In a transition of power presided over by King Charles, Andy Burnham has replaced Keir Starmer as the United Kingdom's prime minister. Both are from the center-left Labour Party.
items.content: In a transition of power presided over by King Charles, Andy Burnham has replaced Keir Starmer as the United Kingdom's prime minister. Both are from the center-left Labour Party. (Image credit: Kirsty Wigglesworth)

items.title: Who is Andy Burnham, the U.K.'s new prime minister?
items.standfirst: On Monday, former Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham succeeds Keir Starmer as the United Kingdom's prime minister. Burnham's politics have been shaped by England's north-south class divide.
items.content: On Monday, former Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham succeeds Keir Starmer as the United Kingdom's prime minister. Burnham's politics have been shaped by England's north-south class divide.(Image credit: Carl Court)

clusters.title: US military identifies two soldiers killed in Iranian attack on Jordan
clusters.neutral_headline: US military identifies two soldiers killed in Iranian attack on Jordan
bullet_summary: military announced it recovered unidentified human remains while searching for an American service member reported missing after an Iranian missile and drone attack in Jordan, as another U
Reported by Guardian World, CBS News, NPR News and others

items.title: US military identifies two soldiers killed in Iranian attack on Jordan
items.standfirst: Defense department says 1Lt Tyler James Feehan of Hawaii and Pvt Isabella Gonzales of Texas were killedThe US military on Monday identified two soldiers killed in action recently as part of the war with Iran.The defense department said in a statement that 1Lt Tyler James Feehan, 25, of Ewa Beach, Hawaii, and Pvt Isabella Gonzales, 19, of Carrollton, Texas, were killed in Jordan, where they were supporting a US mission against the Islamic State group. It’s unclear how they were killed. Continue reading...
items.content: Defense department says 1Lt Tyler James Feehan of Hawaii and Pvt Isabella Gonzales of Texas were killedThe US military on Monday identified two soldiers killed in action recently as part of the war with Iran.The defense department said in a statement that 1Lt Tyler James Feehan, 25, of Ewa Beach, Hawaii, and Pvt Isabella Gonzales, 19, of Carrollton, Texas, were killed in Jordan, where they were supporting a US mission against the Islamic State group. It’s unclear how they were killed. Continue reading...

items.title: U.S. soldiers killed in Iranian attack on base in Jordan identified
items.standfirst: The U.S. military has identified two soldiers killed in an Iranian attack on a U.S. base in Jordan: 1st Lt. Tyler James Feehan, 25, of Ewa Beach, Hawaii, and Pvt. Isabella Gonzales, 19, of Carrollton, Texas. Charlie D'Agata has the latest.
items.content: The U.S. military has identified two soldiers killed in an Iranian attack on a U.S. base in Jordan: 1st Lt. Tyler James Feehan, 25, of Ewa Beach, Hawaii, and Pvt. Isabella Gonzales, 19, of Carrollton, Texas. Charlie D'Agata has the latest.

items.title: Pentagon ID's 2 soldiers killed in Iran strike in Jordan; 1 still missing
items.standfirst: U.S. forces launched a new round of airstrikes against Iran at the direction of President Trump following the deaths of two U.S. soldiers in an Iranian strike in Jordan.
items.content: U.S. forces launched a new round of airstrikes against Iran at the direction of President Trump following the deaths of two U.S. soldiers in an Iranian strike in Jordan.

items.title: U.S. vows to "swiftly punish" Iran as at least 3 troops killed
items.standfirst: At least 3 U.S. service members were killed over the weekend in Iraq and Jordan, where back-to-back Iranian attacks on a U.S. base seem to have evaded defenses.
items.content: At least 3 U.S. service members were killed over the weekend in Iraq and Jordan, where back-to-back Iranian attacks on a U.S. base seem to have evaded defenses.

items.title: U.S. and Iran launch new strikes after 3 U.S. service members are killed
items.standfirst: The U.S. attacks were the ninth consecutive day of bombing since peace talks between the two countries collapsed in dispute over a memorandum of understanding focused on the Strait of Hormuz.
items.content: The U.S. attacks were the ninth consecutive day of bombing since peace talks between the two countries collapsed in dispute over a memorandum of understanding focused on the Strait of Hormuz.(Image credit: Atta Kenare)

items.title: American service member killed in Iraq; U.S. hits Iran for 9th straight night
items.standfirst: Two U.S. service members were killed earlier in Jordan and a third was missing in action, the U.S. Central Command said.
items.content: Two U.S. service members were killed earlier in Jordan and a third was missing in action, the U.S. Central Command said.

items.title: U.S. service member killed in Northern Iraq, says U.S. Central Command
items.standfirst: The U.S. military announced it recovered unidentified human remains while searching for an American service member reported missing after an Iranian missile and drone attack in Jordan, as another U.S. service member was killed in a separate incident in Iraq. NBC News' Julie Tsirkin and Keir Simmons report.
items.content: The U.S. military announced it recovered unidentified human remains while searching for an American service member reported missing after an Iranian missile and drone attack in Jordan, as another U.S. service member was killed in a separate incident in Iraq. NBC News' Julie Tsirkin and Keir Simmons report.

items.title: U.S. military identifies soldiers killed in Iranian attack in Jordan
items.standfirst: The Pentagon on Monday released the names of two soldiers killed in Iranian strikes in Jordan, identifying them as 25-year-old Tyler James Feehan of Hawaii and 19-year-old Isabella Gonzales of Texas.
items.content: The Pentagon on Monday released the names of two soldiers killed in Iranian strikes in Jordan, identifying them as 25-year-old Tyler James Feehan of Hawaii and 19-year-old Isabella Gonzales of Texas.

items.title: US soldier killed and one injured after Iranian attack in Iraq
items.standfirst: The US military reports another death a day after two of its soldiers were killed in Jordan.
items.content: The US military reports another death a day after two of its soldiers were killed in Jordan.

items.title: U.S. and Iran trade strikes after two U.S. service members killed in Jordan

items.content: Two U.S. service members were killed Friday while defending against an Iranian ballistic missile and drone attack in Jordan, and another service member is missing, U.S.

clusters.title: Why are thousands of people protesting in Delhi?
clusters.neutral_headline: Why are thousands of people protesting in Delhi
bullet_summary: Reported by BBC World, NBC World, Guardian World and others

items.title: Why are thousands of people protesting in Delhi?
items.standfirst: Thousands of mostly young people have marched on India's parliament to demand changes to how the education system is run.
items.content: Thousands of mostly young people have marched on India's parliament to demand changes to how the education system is run.

items.title: India’s Cockroach Janta Party movement grows as thousands of protesters gather
items.standfirst: India’s Cockroach Janta Party movement grows as thousands of protesters gather for a march demanding education reforms. The cockroach movement began after national medical school entrance exams were leaked.
items.content: India’s Cockroach Janta Party movement grows as thousands of protesters gather for a march demanding education reforms. The cockroach movement began after national medical school entrance exams were leaked.

items.title: Tens of thousands of Cockroach party protesters defy police with Delhi march
items.standfirst: Youth-led movement calls on education minister to quit in one of India’s largest anti-government rallies in yearsTens of thousands of protesters have defied police orders and descended on Delhi to demand the resignation of India’s education minister in one of the largest anti-government demonstrations in years.A huge crowd, largely made up of students and gen Z-ers, filled the streets in the heart of India’s capital from early Monday morning shouting anti-government slogans. Hundreds had already camped out overnight. Continue reading...
items.content: Youth-led movement calls on education minister to quit in one of India’s largest anti-government rallies in yearsTens of thousands of protesters have defied police orders and descended on Delhi to demand the resignation of India’s education minister in one of the largest anti-government demonstrations in years.A huge crowd, largely made up of students and gen Z-ers, filled the streets in the heart of India’s capital from early Monday morning shouting anti-government slogans. Hundreds had already camped out overnight. Continue reading...

items.title: Photos: Police fire tear gas at India’s Cockroach movement supporters
items.standfirst: The youth-led Cockroach Janta Party protests in New Delhi, demanding India's education minister resign over exam leaks.
items.content: The youth-led Cockroach Janta Party protests in New Delhi, demanding India's education minister resign over exam leaks.

items.title: From hundreds to thousands: How India's 'cockroach' protest movement has grown
items.standfirst: BBC's Azadeh Moshiri reports as thousands attempt to march to India's parliament in support of the Cockroach Janta Party's demand for education reforms.
items.content: BBC's Azadeh Moshiri reports as thousands attempt to march to India's parliament in support of the Cockroach Janta Party's demand for education reforms.

clusters.title: Taylor Farms lettuce sample was false positive for cyclospora, FDA says
clusters.neutral_headline: Taylor Farms lettuce sample was false positive for cyclospora, FDA says
bullet_summary: The FDA said that as of Sunday, it had not found any "confirmed positive sample results for product testing for Cyclospora
The FDA says a sample of Taylor Farms shredded iceberg lettuce that it initially reported positive for the parasite cyclospora was a false positive
As of Sunday, there were ⁠no confirmed positive sample results for cyclospora, the FDA added
The FDA on Saturday had said that a ⁠sample of shredded iceberg lettuce supplied by Taylor Farms tested positive ​for the parasitic illness that has ‌sickened thousands of ‌people in the US

items.title: Taylor Farms lettuce sample was false positive for cyclospora, FDA says
items.standfirst: The FDA said that as of Sunday, it had not found any "confirmed positive sample results for product testing for Cyclospora."
items.content: The FDA said that as of Sunday, it had not found any "confirmed positive sample results for product testing for Cyclospora."

items.title: Sample of Taylor Farms lettuce that tested positive for cyclospora was false positive, FDA says
items.standfirst: The FDA says a sample of Taylor Farms shredded iceberg lettuce that it initially reported positive for the parasite cyclospora was a false positive. The outbreak has sickened more than 1,600 people across five states. Ian Lee reports.
items.content: The FDA says a sample of Taylor Farms shredded iceberg lettuce that it initially reported positive for the parasite cyclospora was a false positive. The outbreak has sickened more than 1,600 people across five states. Ian Lee reports.

items.title: FDA says review of Taylor Farms lettuce shows cyclospora finding was false positive
items.standfirst: Spokesperson for Taylor Fresh Foods told Reuters ‌that the company was informed FDA made a mistakeWhat is the parasitic illness cyclosporiasis?How to properly clean produce to help avoid cyclosporaThe US Food and Drug ⁠Administration said on Sunday that laboratory experts re-checked lettuce sample results from Taylor Farms in an ⁠investigation into an ⁠outbreak of the ​cyclospora parasite and concluded the finding should be considered a false positive.As of Sunday, there were ⁠no confirmed positive sample results for cyclospora, the FDA added. The FDA on Saturday had said that a ⁠sample of shredded iceberg lettuce supplied by Taylor Farms tested positive ​for the parasitic illness that has ‌sickened thousands of ‌people in the US.Guardian staff contributed Continue reading...
items.content: Spokesperson for Taylor Fresh Foods told Reuters ‌that the company was informed FDA made a mistakeWhat is the parasitic illness cyclosporiasis?How to properly clean produce to help avoid cyclosporaThe US Food and Drug ⁠Administration said on Sunday that laboratory experts re-checked lettuce sample results from Taylor Farms in an ⁠investigation into an ⁠outbreak of the ​cyclospora parasite and concluded the finding should be considered a false positive.As of Sunday, there were ⁠no confirmed positive sample results for cyclospora, the FDA added. The FDA on Saturday had said that a ⁠sample of shredded iceberg lettuce supplied by Taylor Farms tested positive ​for the parasitic illness that has ‌sickened thousands of ‌people in the US.Guardian staff contributed Continue reading...

items.title: Cyclosporiasis outbreak sparks race to remove contaminated lettuce
items.standfirst: A cyclospora outbreak, which has sickened thousands of people nationwide, is believed to have been traced to contaminated lettuce from Mexico supplied by produce giant Taylor Farms. Ian Lee has the latest.
items.content: A cyclospora outbreak, which has sickened thousands of people nationwide, is believed to have been traced to contaminated lettuce from Mexico supplied by produce giant Taylor Farms. Ian Lee has the latest.

items.title: At least 930 people have died in Congo's Ebola outbreak
items.standfirst: Congo's health ministry said the deaths were recorded out of the 2,344 cases confirmed as of Saturday. Thirty-seven new deaths were recorded in the previous 24 hours in one of the highest totals.
items.content: Congo's health ministry said the deaths were recorded out of the 2,344 cases confirmed as of Saturday. Thirty-seven new deaths were recorded in the previous 24 hours in one of the highest totals.(Image credit: Moses Sawasawa)

items.title: Ebola outbreak fastest growing epidemic on record as deaths rise again

clusters.title: Russia launches major ballistic missile attack on Ukrainian cities
clusters.neutral_headline: Russia launches major ballistic missile attack on Ukrainian cities
bullet_summary: Ukrainian officials said the capital had been hit with about 40 Iskander-M and hypersonic Zircon missiles
Reported by BBC World, Guardian World

items.title: Russia launches major ballistic missile attack on Ukrainian cities
items.standfirst: Eight people die and dozens are injured as several cities are hit by missile and drone attacks, while Kyiv continues its own assault on Russian infrastructure.
items.content: Eight people die and dozens are injured as several cities are hit by missile and drone attacks, while Kyiv continues its own assault on Russian infrastructure.

items.title: Russia pounds Kyiv for five hours in one of its biggest ballistic missile attacks
items.standfirst: Attack on Ukraine’s capital and other cities leaves at least six people dead and dozens injuredRussia has carried out one of its biggest ballistic missile attacks on Kyiv and other Ukrainian cities, launching a five-hour assault that left at least six people dead and dozens injured, and caused fires and damage across the capital.Ukrainian officials said the capital had been hit with about 40 Iskander-M and hypersonic Zircon missiles. Residents heard an air raid siren at 1.30am. There was the sound of air defences, followed minutes later by a series of booms and explosions. Continue reading...
items.content: Attack on Ukraine’s capital and other cities leaves at least six people dead and dozens injuredRussia has carried out one of its biggest ballistic missile attacks on Kyiv and other Ukrainian cities, launching a five-hour assault that left at least six people dead and dozens injured, and caused fires and damage across the capital.Ukrainian officials said the capital had been hit with about 40 Iskander-M and hypersonic Zircon missiles. Residents heard an air raid siren at 1.30am. There was the sound of air defences, followed minutes later by a series of booms and explosions. Continue reading...
